name: GR 123: Plentzia and Gorliz Coastal Loop
name_original: Gaztelumendi desde Plentzia — circular por el faro de Cabo Villano
description: Hike the challenging 11.3-mile Gaztelumendi circular route from Plentzia, featuring coastal views, the Gorliz Lighthouse, and historical bun
region_intro: Hiking around Plentzia offers a diverse landscape where the Plentzia River meets the Cantabrian Sea, creating a scenic bay and estuary. The region features varied terrain, including coastal paths, cliffs, and wooded areas, extending into the Uribe Kosta. Elevations are generally moderate, with some routes offering significant climbs for panoramic views. Best hiking trails around Plentzia The most popular hiking route is GR 123: Plentzia and Gorliz Coastal Loop, a difficult 11.3 miles (18.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 36 minutes to complete. This challenging route offers extensive coastal vistas. Another top favourite among local hikers is Gorliz Lighthouse – View of Billano Island loop from Plentzia, a moderate 8.0 miles (12.8 km) path. This route provides views of Billano Island and the Gorliz Lighthouse. Local hikers also love the Plentzia Pedestrian Bridge – Butrón Castle loop from Plentzia, a 9.9 miles (16.0 km) trail leading through urban and natural settings, often completed in about 4 hours 27 minutes. Hiking around Plentzia is defined by coastal paths, river estuaries, and varied Uribe Kosta landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging coastal loops. The routes in Plentzia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 170 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Plentzia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Plentzia?
A: Hiking around Plentzia offers a diverse mix of coastal beauty and inland charm. You'll encounter scenic Plentzia Bay and its estuary, dramatic cliffs along the Cantabrian Sea, and verdant wooded areas. The region is part of the Uribe Kosta, providing varied terrain from seaside paths to more rural settings.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for families or casual strolls?
A: Yes, Plentzia offers several easy walks. The &lt;a href="https://visitplentzia.eus/en/what-to-do/plentzia-neigbourhoods/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plentzia Neighborhoods Hike&lt;/a&gt; is a pleasant, low-difficulty option that takes you through urban areas, rural settings, and offers beach views. There are 7 easy routes in total, perfect for all skill levels.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features to look out for on the trails?
A: Many trails offer views of significant landmarks and natural features. You can see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Butrón Castle&lt;/a&gt; on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1231997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plentzia Pedestrian Bridge – Butrón Castle loop from Plentzia&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40336698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorliz Lighthouse – View of Billano Island loop from Plentzia&lt;/a&gt; provides views of Billano Island and the Gorliz Lighthouse itself. The Plentzia River estuary, with its dramatic tidal changes, is also a unique natural attraction.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046096282/gr-123-plentzia-and-gorliz-coastal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 123: Plentzia and Gorliz Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.2 km (11.3 miles) route with a 630-meter elevation gain, offering extensive coastal vistas. This challenging trail takes approximately 5 hours and 36 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Plentzia?
A: Yes, Plentzia is well-suited for circular routes. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046096282/gr-123-plentzia-and-gorliz-coastal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 123: Plentzia and Gorliz Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40336698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorliz Lighthouse – View of Billano Island loop from Plentzia&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31920041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muriola Beach – Txitxarropunta Cliffs loop from Plentzia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Plentzia?
A: The hiking routes in Plentzia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 170 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse coastal paths, the beauty of the river estuary, and the varied Uribe Kosta landscapes.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the sea and cliffs?
A: Many trails provide stunning coastal and cliff views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046096282/gr-123-plentzia-and-gorliz-coastal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 123: Plentzia and Gorliz Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt; is particularly known for its extensive coastal vistas. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31920041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muriola Beach – Txitxarropunta Cliffs loop from Plentzia&lt;/a&gt;, which specifically highlights these features.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Plentzia?
A: Plentzia's diverse landscapes make it suitable for hiking throughout much of the year. The protected nature of Plentzia Bay means its beach is ideal for swimming even when Atlantic waves are high, suggesting pleasant conditions for coastal walks during warmer months. However, the varied terrain means you can find enjoyable routes in most seasons.

Q: How long do hikes around Plentzia typically take?
A: Hiking durations vary significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. Easy strolls, like the Plentzia Neighborhoods Hike, can be around 2 hours. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31880669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorliz Lighthouse – Plentzia Bay loop from Plentzia&lt;/a&gt;, might take around 3 hours 40 minutes. More challenging trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046096282/gr-123-plentzia-and-gorliz-coastal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 123: Plentzia and Gorliz Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 5 hours.

Q: Are there opportunities to explore historical sites while hiking?
A: Yes, some routes combine natural beauty with historical interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1231997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plentzia Pedestrian Bridge – Butrón Castle loop from Plentzia&lt;/a&gt; leads towards the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Butrón Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/566248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Askorriaga Fortress&lt;/a&gt; are another historical site in the area that can be explored.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Plentzia area?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes available around Plentzia, catering to various skill levels. This includes 7 easy routes, 14 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes, ensuring options for everyone from casual walkers to experienced trekkers.
tips: From this modest summit of just 127 meters above sea level we will have views over the entire Plentzia bay, its rocky outcrops and its beach.
On this cape are the ruins of the Askorriaga fortress, built in the 18th century. Although few vestiges remain of this building, the views of the sea are still privileged.

Built in 1990, this lighthouse holds the title of being the highest altitude lighthouse not only in Euskadi, but also in the Cantabrian Sea.
From this point, we will also have privileged views over the Bay of Plentzia.
Just below are some bunkers built in the post-war period.
you have to go down a very narrow path

We will find the geodesic vertex on top of a small stone construction.
From this area we can see Fraile Island (also known as Billano Irla or Villano Island).
This vantage point overlooking the Cantabrian Sea has very easy access. You can get here through three steps that lead to a gently sloping ramp.

faqs.question: Is the Gaztelumendi from Plentzia — circular via the Cabo Villano lighthouse trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult, requiring very good fitness levels, sure-footedness, and sturdy shoes. While some sections are easily accessible, the overall length (18.2 km) and elevation gain (630 m) make it challenging. It's not ideal for beginners seeking an easy stroll, but experienced hikers will find it rewarding.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Gaztelumendi from Plentzia circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring a mix of surfaces. You'll encounter significant portions of unpaved (7.64 km) and natural (1.06 km) paths, along with some paved (2.15 km) and asphalt (1.20 km) sections. There's also a small amount of gravel. Be prepared for potentially muddy and slippery conditions on unpaved sections, especially around Kukullu, if it has rained recently.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Gaztelumendi from Plentzia circular trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery and historical sites. Key highlights include the View of Plentzia Bay from Astondo, the Ruins of Askorriaga Fortress, the modern Gorliz Lighthouse (the highest altitude lighthouse in Euskadi), and the Gorliz Coastal Bunkers. You'll also pass the Ermua Geodetic Point and Viewpoint and the Gaztelumendi Viewpoint, both offering expansive coastal views.

faqs.question: Where can I park for this circular hike?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Plentzia, being the starting and ending point, offers various parking options within the town. It's advisable to look for public parking near the town center or along the bay, keeping in mind that it can get busy, especially during peak season.

faqs.question: How can I reach the trailhead by public transport?
faqs.answer: Plentzia is accessible by public transport. You can typically reach Plentzia via metro or bus services from Bilbao, which is well-connected to the region. Check local transport schedules for the most up-to-date information on routes and timings to Plentzia.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Cantabrian coast can be enjoyed year-round, but spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant weather for hiking, with milder temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or heavy rain. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring cooler, wetter conditions, potentially making unpaved sections muddier.

faqs.question: Is the Gaztelumendi from Plentzia — circular via the Cabo Villano lighthouse trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, coastal trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially near cliffs, livestock, or in more populated areas. Be mindful of other hikers and local regulations. Ensure you carry enough water for your dog, as water sources on the trail may be limited.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. It is a public access route along the coast and through natural areas.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, essential gear includes sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support, appropriate clothing layers for changing weather, rain gear, plenty of water, snacks, a map or GPS device, and sun protection. Trekking poles can be helpful on steeper or slippery sections.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this 18.2 km circular hike is approximately 5 hours and 36 minutes. However, this can vary depending on your fitness level, the number of stops you make for photos or breaks, and weather conditions.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with and follows sections of several official trails, including the Bizkaiko bira (71% of the route), and various segments of the Gr123.

waypoints.name: View of Plentzia Bay from Astondo
